title: Pega Platform '25 — Get Started with Low-Code Application Development
content:
# Pega Platform '25 — Get Started with Low-Code Application Development

## Overview
Plan, design, and build efficient applications by applying the principles of low-code application development. With Pega Platform '25, you can create goal-oriented applications that are easy to build, maintain, and update. Low-code development takes advantage of these key principles: focus on customer goals, Pega Blueprint for IT-business collaboration, AI-powered tools (Pega GenAI), out-of-the-box functionalities, and a low-code authoring environment (App Studio).

## Key Principles of Low-Code Development

### Focus on Customer Goals
Create purpose-built applications focused on one goal at a time. For example, for an HR department, deliver separate applications for hiring and employee feedback. This builds granular software adjustable to changing business goals.

### Pega Blueprint
Pega Blueprint is a powerful GenAI-supported tool that closes the gap between IT and business. It helps businesses prototype applications visually regardless of technical expertise. You provide basic information about your application's purpose, and Blueprint generates a draft with suggested workflows, data, Personas, and device previews. Access it at https://pega.com/blueprint.

### IT and Business Collaboration with AI Tools
After prototyping in Blueprint, low-code Pega tools help IT and business tailor the application. Pega GenAI features optimize and accelerate work at both design time (building) and run time (user interaction). GenAI automates actions and provides conversational support in natural language.

### Out-of-the-Box Functionalities
Pega Platform offers default solutions that jump-start development. Using OOTB features saves resources, accelerates delivery, and facilitates smooth updates. For example, Pega automatically creates UIs based on low-code configurations.

### Low-Code Authoring Environment (App Studio)
App Studio enables team members to create solutions hiding complexity behind the scenes. Both IT and business representatives can use visual tools to implement software solutions. App Studio supports prototyping and creation with Pega Blueprint.

### Modules
Constellation supports modules — mini applications holding single functionality that are reusable across your business. Examples: Document, Customer, Risk, Quote, Meeting modules. Each module works as a separate data unit integrated with a System of Record (SOR) and can include reusable Views and smart shapes. Modules are added to application Rulesets and become available to Business Architects and Citizen Developers in App Studio.

## Center-Out Design with Blueprint

### What Is Center-Out?
Center-out is a unique architectural approach that places business logic at the center of application design while maintaining clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. It enables organizations to start with customer needs and build flexible, scalable solutions.

### Understanding Center-Out
- Focuses on customer outcomes first, enabling quick adaptation to changing market demands
- Maintains enterprise-scale capabilities without compromising system integrity
- Blueprint helps bring the Center-out philosophy to life with tools that automatically generate outcome-oriented workflows

### Blueprint for Constellation
- Constellation encourages building a comprehensive Data Model from the start because the UI is rendered automatically based on Data Model and developer configuration
- Blueprint generates a complete and correct model of Case and Data structures based on Pega best practices
- Constellation separates UI presentation and orchestration layers from business logic and data
- Uses model-driven APIs so you can use the same assets across channels

## Creating a Blueprint — Step by Step

### Step 1: Define Application Context
1. Go to https://www.pega.com/blueprint and click Build a Blueprint
2. Click Create a Blueprint on My Blueprint Dashboard
3. Select industry and sub-industry tiles matching your business context
4. Select Department/function that matches your business process
5. Optional: Upload supporting documents (PDF, DOCX, MOV, MP4, JPG, PNG, DDL, OpenAPI, AWS Transform, Google MAT, BPMN files)
6. Describe the application purpose and functional description
7. Select the language for Blueprint generation
8. Optional: Set geographic location context

### Step 2: Configure Case Types
- Review AI-generated Case Types in the Workflows step
- Add new Case Types or edit existing ones
- Each Case Type needs a name and description reflecting the business process
- Optional: Upload BPMN files to build lifecycle from business process diagrams
- Click Generate More to auto-create additional Case Types with AI
- Save and Regenerate Case Lifecycle to apply changes

### Step 3: Configure Case Lifecycles
- Customize the path business processes follow to resolution in the Workflow Details step
- Review and refine Stages and Processes for each Case Type
- Add Steps: Collect information, Approve/Reject, Automation, Create Case, Decision, Generate Document, Generative AI, Robotic Automation, Send Notification
- Add Step descriptions (displayed as tooltips)
- Create Alternate Stages for exception handling (e.g., rejected cases)

### Step 4: Configure Data Model
- Define fields that Case Types capture and display at run time
- Field types: Text, Boolean, Currency, DateTime, Date, Decimal, Email, Integer, Percentage, Phone, Time, URL
- Fields can reference Data Objects (single or multiple values)
- Field value sources: User input, Calculated (business logic), or Integration (external)
- Primary Fields represent critical data visible whenever interacting with a Case
- Use Generate More to auto-create fields with generative AI

### Step 5: Configure Data Objects and Integrations
- Data Objects represent business entities (customer, product, etc.)
- Review AI-generated Data Objects in the Data & Integrations step
- Import data definitions from OpenAPI Specification (Swagger/YAML) or DDL files
- Data Objects can be locally sourced (Pega Local) or externally sourced (external SOR)
- Use Generate More to auto-create additional Data Objects

### Step 6: Define Personas
- Personas group users by responsibilities, channel access, and case assignments
- Example: hiring process with Candidate, HR Worker, and Manager Personas
- Add, edit, or generate more Personas with AI
- Each Persona has a name and description of the user type it represents

## Collaborating on Blueprints

### Sharing and Roles
- Blueprint owners can invite collaborators via email
- Two access levels: Editor (can edit) and Viewer (can only view)
- Invitees who aren't registered Pega Community users must register first
- Changes tracked in Blueprint history/changelog

## Creating a New Application from a Blueprint

### Import Process
1. In App Studio header, click application name → New Application
2. Click Build from a Blueprint
3. Upload a .BLUEPRINT file or select an already-uploaded Blueprint
4. Configure application name and optional built-on application for reuse
5. For each Case Type: Build from Blueprint, Inherit from existing, or Do not build
6. For each Data Object: Build from Blueprint, Inherit from existing, Reuse from existing, or Do not build
7. For each Persona: Build from Blueprint or Do not build
8. Review Configurations: application ID, version, base language, org settings, class layers
9. Click Submit — all assets except Access Roles are created in a Branch

### Built-On Application Pattern
- Select an existing application to reuse assets from
- Ensure all rulesets in the built-on application are locked
- Clone existing Case Types or Data Objects from the built-on app
- Default base ruleset password: pega

## Building a Case Type

### Case Lifecycle Elements
- **Stages**: Milestones indicating transitions between departments (e.g., Sourcing → Interview → Verification → Offer → Onboarding)
- **Alternate Stages**: Alternative paths for exceptions (e.g., candidate rejection)
- **Processes**: Series of tasks within a Stage; can be sequential or parallel for faster resolution; reusable across Stages and Case Types
- **Steps**: Smallest elements representing single tasks or Assignments; can be user actions or automations

### Step Types Available
- Collect information (user input forms)
- Approve/Reject (approval workflows)
- Automation (automated system actions)
- Create Case (spawn child or related cases)
- Decision (conditional branching)
- Generate Document (automated document creation)
- Generative AI (AI-powered automation)
- Robotic Automation (RPA integration)
- Send Notification (automated notifications)

## Pega Autopilot — AI-Powered Development Assistant

### Overview
Pega Autopilot is a design-time assistant in App Studio that accelerates application development through conversational AI support.

### Capabilities
- **Conversational support**: Chat-like interface; answers questions with documentation references and wizard launchers; context-aware of application content
- **Case Type creation**: Auto-generates workflows with Stages and Steps based on Case Type name; suggests Embedded Data and data reference fields
- **View population**: Auto-populates empty Views with suggested fields based on Case Type, Stage, and Step names
- **Email content generation**: Generates Send Email Step content based on workflow context; editable and regenerable before submission

### Prerequisites for Autopilot
- Pega GenAI features require Pega Cloud
- Complete GenAI onboarding process
- Enable the EnableGenerativeAI toggle

## Pega Autopilot and Data Designer

### Data Objects Creation
- Autopilot is context-aware when working in Data Designer
- Conversationally instruct Autopilot to create Data Objects with fields
- Autopilot confirms details (field types, storage system) before creating
- Creates one Data Object at a time; enter consecutive requests for multiple

### Integration Creation
- Autopilot creates Data Objects connected to external systems using OpenAPI specifications
- Provides endpoints, descriptions, parameters, and responses
- Can prepare OpenAPI specifications from other available specifications if you don't have one
- Asks for confirmation before each step to minimize errors

## Data Objects and Their Sourcing

### Data Architecture
- Data Objects can be sourced locally or externally
- Data Pages act as intermediaries, keeping the Data Model independent from its sources
- CRUD operations handled by savable Data Pages
- Data mapping ensures correct information storage for externally sourced objects
- Objects can use different databases for transactional (speed-optimized) and reporting (analysis-optimized) purposes

### Integration Landscape
- View all systems of record in the Integration Landscape diagram in App Studio (Data landing page)
- Filtering options for exploring connections between Data Objects and SORs

## Managing Data with Fields

### Field Types
- Field Type defines the format of information (Date Time won't accept strings)
- Available types: Text, Boolean, Currency, DateTime, Date, Decimal, Email, Integer, Percentage, Phone, Time, URL
- Fields can also reference Data Objects (single or multiple values)

### Fields in Data Objects vs. Case Types
- Fields added to a Data Object are available to every Case Type referencing that object
- Fields created directly in a Case are limited to that Case Type only
- Example: Customer Data Object fields become available in any Case Type using a Customer reference field

### Using Blueprint and Autopilot for Data Design
- Blueprint auto-generates complete Data Models based on Pega best practices
- Autopilot supplements Blueprint-based Data Models during development
- Autopilot generates Case Types and Data Models fitting the larger application context

## Exporting an Existing Application as a Blueprint
1. In App Studio navigation pane, click Overview
2. In the "Design for the future" section, click Export as Blueprint
3. Save the .BLUEPRINT file to your device
4. Share with other developers or use as a template for similar applications

## Interview and Certification Q&A

**Q: What is Pega Blueprint and how does it work?**
A: Pega Blueprint is a GenAI-powered tool at https://pega.com/blueprint that closes the gap between IT and business. You provide basic information about your application's purpose, and Blueprint generates a draft with suggested Case Types, workflows, Data Models, Personas, and UI previews. You can edit all AI-generated elements, collaborate with team members (Editor/Viewer roles), upload supporting documents (PDF, DOCX, BPMN, OpenAPI, DDL), and then import the Blueprint into Pega Platform to build the application.

**Q: What is center-out architecture in Pega?**
A: Center-out places business logic at the center of application design with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. You design the case lifecycle and business outcomes first, then extend outward to channels and integrations. Constellation supports this by rendering UI automatically from the Data Model, separating presentation from logic via model-driven APIs, enabling the same assets to work across channels.

**Q: What are the elements of a Case Lifecycle?**
A: A Case Lifecycle consists of Stages (milestones/transitions between departments), Processes (series of tasks within a Stage — can be sequential or parallel), and Steps (smallest elements — single tasks or automations). Step types include: Collect information, Approve/Reject, Automation, Create Case, Decision, Generate Document, Generative AI, Robotic Automation, and Send Notification. Alternate Stages handle exceptions.

**Q: What is Pega Autopilot and what can it do?**
A: Pega Autopilot is an AI design-time assistant in App Studio. It provides: conversational support with documentation references, automated Case Type creation with AI-generated workflows and fields, View population with suggested fields, email content generation for Send Email Steps, and Data Object creation via natural language instructions. It requires Pega Cloud and the EnableGenerativeAI toggle.

**Q: How do you create an application from a Blueprint?**
A: In App Studio, click New Application → Build from a Blueprint → upload .BLUEPRINT file. Then configure: (1) Case Types — build from Blueprint, inherit from existing, or skip. (2) Data Objects — build, inherit, reuse from existing, or skip. (3) Personas — build or skip. (4) Review configurations (app ID, version, org settings, class layers). Click Submit. All assets except Access Roles are created in a Branch.

**Q: What are Modules in Constellation?**
A: Modules are mini applications holding single reusable functionality across your business. Examples include Document, Customer, Risk, Quote, and Meeting modules. Each module works as a separate data unit integrated with a SOR, can include reusable Views and smart shapes. After developers add a module to a Ruleset, it becomes available to Business Architects and Citizen Developers in App Studio.

**Q: How does data sourcing work for Data Objects?**
A: Data Objects can be locally sourced (Pega Local) or externally sourced from SORs connected via integrations. Data Pages act as intermediaries keeping the Data Model independent from sources. CRUD operations use savable Data Pages. Objects can use separate databases for transactional (speed) and reporting (analysis) purposes. Import data definitions from OpenAPI (Swagger/YAML) or DDL files.
